title: Else & Else If 🔀
description: Handle alternative paths when conditions are false.

slides.title: Plan B 🛡️
slides.content:
An `if` statement is great, but what if we want something *else* to happen when the condition is `false`?

Instead of just skipping the block and doing nothing, we can provide a backup plan: **The `else` statement**.

slides.title: If / Else Syntax 🔀
slides.content:
The `else` block attaches directly to the end of the `if` block's closing brace `}`.

If the condition is True, it runs block A. If it's False, it runs block B.

quizData.question: Can an `else` block have its own condition like `else (score < 50)`?

options.text: No, it just acts as a catch-all.

quizData.explanation: A plain 'else' does NOT take a condition. It simply catches everything that failed the 'if' check.

slides.title: Multiple Paths: Else If 🚦
slides.content:
Sometimes there are more than two paths. A grade isn't just Pass/Fail; it could be A, B, C, D, or F.

We can chain multiple checks together using **`else if`**.

slides.title: Chaining Checks ⛓️
slides.content: Java checks these in order from top to bottom. The **very first** one that is `true` gets executed, and Java skips the rest.

quizData.question: If `temperature = 90`, which blocks will run?
quizData.explanation: Even though 90 > 60 is true, Java STOPS checking as soon as it hits the first true condition (`90 > 80`).
